question_title: Will EUR/GBP fall below 0.82 before May 1, 2026?
summary: EUR/GBP currently at 0.8642 (ECB reference rate 0.86503) needs to fall 5.1% to breach 0.82 within ~34 trading days remaining before May 1. This is a 2.25-sigma move at typical EUR/GBP volatility (~6.3% annualized) and 3.38-sigma at recent empirical vol (~4.2%). The 52-week low (0.8315) is itself still 1.4% above the target — the pair hasn't traded at 0.82 since pre-Brexit era (2015-2016). All analyst forecasts project EUR/GBP in the 0.85-0.88 range. Monte Carlo simulation with blended volatility scenarios gives ~4.5% probability. While there's a recent downward trend (GBP strengthening), the magnitude required is very large for this timeframe.

factors.description: EUR/GBP currently at 0.8642, needs to fall 5.1% to reach 0.82 — a very large move for this low-vol pair in just 34 trading days
52-week low is 0.8315, still 1.4% above the 0.82 target — the rate would need to break through multi-year support
Recent downward trend: EUR/GBP declined from 0.877 to 0.864 over 2 weeks (~1.5%), showing some GBP strengthening momentum
Analyst consensus forecasts EUR/GBP at 0.85-0.88 through Q2 2026 — no analyst forecasts 0.82
Monte Carlo simulation: at typical EUR/GBP vol (~6.3% annualized), probability of touching 0.82 is ~2.5%; at empirical recent vol (~4.2%), it's ~0.1%
Potential for vol spike from unexpected BoE/ECB policy divergence or macro shock could increase probability but would need to be very large

reasoning:
**Forecast: 4% probability (NO strongly favored)**

Key findings:
- **Current rate**: ECB EUR/GBP at 0.865, needing a 5.1% decline to breach 0.82
- **Historical context**: The 52-week low (0.8315) is still above the target; EUR/GBP hasn't been at 0.82 since 2015-2016
- **Volatility analysis**: At typical EUR/GBP vol (~6.3% annualized), this requires a ~2.25-sigma move in 34 trading days
- **Monte Carlo**: Blended across vol scenarios gives ~4.5% probability of touching 0.82
- **Analyst consensus**: All forecasts place EUR/GBP at 0.85-0.88 through Q2 2026

The only realistic path to YES would be a major macro shock (e.g., ECB emergency rate cut, dramatic UK rate hike, or eurozone crisis) causing a vol regime shift — an unlikely but non-zero tail scenario.
sources_consulted: [(Polymarket) NBA: Will the Heat beat the Wizards by more than 7.5 points in their November 18th matchup?](https://polymarket.com/event/nba-will-the-heat-beat-the-wizards-by-more-than-7pt5-points-in-their-november-18th-matchup)

[Euro to Pound Forecast 2026: EUR/GBP Expected to Reach 0.88500](https://www.exchangerates.org.uk/currency-forecasts/euro-to-pound-forecast)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if the ECB EUR/GBP reference rate (GBP per EUR) is below 0.82 on at least one date on or after February 24, 2026 and before May 1, 2026. This question resolves to no if the ECB EUR/GBP reference rate is never below 0.82 in that period. The rate series will be taken from the ECB reference-rate page at https://www.ecb.europa.eu/stats/policy_and_exchange_rates/euro_reference_exchange_rates/html/eurofxref-graph-gbp.en.html
fine_print: Use the ECB reference rate as displayed for each date (do not substitute other intraday prices). If the ECB page is unavailable, resolve using credible sources per https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions Candidate Sources: ECB euro reference rates data feed (backup) https://www.ecb.europa.eu/stats/eurofxref/eurofxref-daily.xml| Bank of England daily spot exchange rate dataset (backup) https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/boeapps/database/Rates.asp
background_info:
This forecasts a notable sterling-strength threshold that matters for UK inflation, BoE expectations, and cross-border flows.
